title: centaurea axillaris var. ochroleuca
intro: Centaurea axillaris var. ochroleuca, also known as yellow mountain cornflower, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of Centaurea axillaris var. ochroleuca may help alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, offering potential health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots can be used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
